name: Iced Molasses Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together molasses, white sugar, shortening, and hot water until thoroughly combined.
2. In a separate bowl, combine baking soda, ground ginger, cinnamon, and 4 cups of sifted flour.
3. Mix the dry ingredients into the liquids, stirring to blend well.
4. Gradually add more flour until the dough is stiff enough to roll.
5. Chill the dough for 1-2 hours before baking.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Grease cookie sheets and roll out the dough to about 1/6-inch thick.
8. Cut shapes with your favorite cookie cutter.
9. Bake for 8-11 minutes until done, then cool completely.
10. Frost with decorative frosting. Cookies can also be dusted with decorative sugar or sprinkles before baking.
11. To make frosting, beat egg whites until frothy, then beat in confectioners' sugar and vanilla. If desired, divide the icing into several bowls and add a drop or two of food coloring. For yellow frosting, add 1/2 teaspoon lemon extract.
